reasoning: Event count datasets (like ACLED riots) are typically overdispersed and right-skewed, meaning the mean is inflated by a few months with massive spikes, and the median month falls below the mean. The 360-day average of 11.833 was likely inflated by peak summer protest periods (e.g., massive August 2025 ceasefire and hostage protests). While we found a few instances of recent riots (Haredi draft clashes on Oct 22, a soccer riot on Oct 19), October 2025 has not featured the same nationwide, daily violent clashes seen during the peak summer months. Because the 30-day period for the Nov 2 resolution date is almost entirely in the past and does not show an abnormal spike in news reports, it is very likely to fall below the 12-riot threshold required to exceed the annual average. For the later dates, uncertainty gradually increases, pulling the probability closer to 0.5.
